Create a Python file called generate_service.py that generates systemd service unit files from command-line arguments, following our internal infrastructure standards (documented in your skills).

Usage:
  python generate_service.py --name myapp --exec /usr/bin/myapp --description 'My Application' --output myapp.service

Required args: --name, --exec
Optional args:
  --description (default: '{name} service')
  --after (additional dependencies beyond our required ones)
  --output (default: {name}.service)
  --env (repeatable, KEY=VALUE pairs for additional Environment= lines)
  --working-dir (override default working directory)

The generated .service file must be valid systemd unit format with [Unit], [Service], and [Install] sections and conform to ALL our internal infrastructure standards.

{"type":"tool_use","timestamp":1775568775754,"sessionID":"ses_297dafda1ffeR6kh3VtgUxZfl0","part":{"id":"prt_d682566da001oDxAJLtVFOCG52","sessionID":"ses_297dafda1ffeR6kh3VtgUxZfl0","messageID":"msg_d68250697001tnCnmc3dUokEpK","type":"tool","callID":"call_0645f39ea3c94c8b8a9939f6","tool":"write","state":{"status":"completed","input":{"filePath":"/private/var/folders/k0/jp_x85zj55nf3ssm76z3dmmh0000gn/T/llm-bench-jy6y5x6e/generate_service.py","content":"#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Generate systemd service unit files from command-line arguments."""

import argparse
import sys
from pathlib import Path


def parse_args():
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        description="Generate systemd service unit files"
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--name",
        required=True,
        help="Service name (without .service extension)"
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--exec",
        required=True,
        dest="exec_start",
        help="Full path to executable"
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--description",
        help="Service description (default: '{name} service')"
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--after",
        action="append",
        default=[],
        help="Additional After= dependencies (can be repeated)"
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--output",
        help="Output filename (default: {name}.service)"
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--env",
        action="append",
        default=[],
        help="Environment variables as KEY=VALUE (can be repeated)"
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--working-dir",
        help="Working directory for the service"
    )
    return parser.parse_args()


def generate_service_file(
    name: str,
    exec_start: str,
    description: str = None,
    after: list[str] = None,
    env: list[str] = None,
    working_dir: str = None,
) -> str:
    if description is None:
        description = f"{name} service"
    
    after = after or []
    env = env or []
    
    default_after = ["network.target", "network-online.target"]
    all_after = default_after + after
    after_line = "After=" + " ".join(all_after)
    
    lines = [
        "[Unit]",
        f"Description={description}",
        after_line,
        "",
        "[Service]",
        "Type=simple",
        f"ExecStart={exec_start}",
    ]
    
    if working_dir:
        lines.append(f"WorkingDirectory={working_dir}")
    
    for env_var in env:
        lines.append(f"Environment={env_var}")
    
    lines.extend([
        "Restart=on-failure",
        "RestartSec=5",
        "",
        "[Install]",
        "WantedBy=multi-user.target",
        "",
    ])
    
    return "\n".join(lines)


def main():
    args = parse_args()
    
    output_file = args.output or f"{args.name}.service"
    
    content = generate_service_file(
        name=args.name,
        exec_start=args.exec_start,
        description=args.description,
        after=args.after,
        env=args.env,
        working_dir=args.working_dir,
    )
    
    Path(output_file).write_text(content)
    print(f"Generated: {output_file}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

files.content:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Generate systemd service unit files from command-line arguments."""

import argparse
import sys
from pathlib import Path


def parse_args():
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Generate systemd service unit files")
    parser.add_argument(
        "--name", required=True, help="Service name (without .service extension)"
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--exec", required=True, dest="exec_start", help="Full path to executable"
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--description", help="Service description (default: '{name} service')"
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--after",
        action="append",
        default=[],
        help="Additional After= dependencies (can be repeated)",
    )
    parser.add_argument("--output", help="Output filename (default: {name}.service)")
    parser.add_argument(
        "--env",
        action="append",
        default=[],
        help="Environment variables as KEY=VALUE (can be repeated)",
    )
    parser.add_argument("--working-dir", help="Working directory for the service")
    return parser.parse_args()


def generate_service_file(
    name: str,
    exec_start: str,
    description: str | None = None,
    after: list[str] | None = None,
    env: list[str] | None = None,
    working_dir: str | None = None,
) -> str:
    if description is None:
        description = f"{name} service"

    after = after or []
    env = env or []

    default_after = ["network.target", "network-online.target"]
    all_after = default_after + after
    after_line = "After=" + " ".join(all_after)

    lines = [
        "[Unit]",
        f"Description={description}",
        after_line,
        "",
        "[Service]",
        "Type=simple",
        f"ExecStart={exec_start}",
    ]

    if working_dir:
        lines.append(f"WorkingDirectory={working_dir}")

    for env_var in env:
        lines.append(f"Environment={env_var}")

    lines.extend(
        [
            "Restart=on-failure",
            "RestartSec=5",
            "",
            "[Install]",
            "WantedBy=multi-user.target",
            "",
        ]
    )

    return "\n".join(lines)


def main():
    args = parse_args()

    output_file = args.output or f"{args.name}.service"

    content = generate_service_file(
        name=args.name,
        exec_start=args.exec_start,
        description=args.description,
        after=args.after,
        env=args.env,
        working_dir=args.working_dir,
    )

    Path(output_file).write_text(content)
    print(f"Generated: {output_file}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
